heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
### Numeric thresholds (consolidated)
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary
- **Consistency note:** All falsifiability thresholds are identical across CONCEPT.md and THEORY.md. Any future updates must be synchronised across both files.

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body:
- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: 2026-08-01; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time).
- **Consistency note:** The set of secondary outcomes listed here (MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L, hospitalisations) must match exactly the set in THEORY.md. Currently, THEORY.md includes 'physician time per visit' as an additional secondary outcome; this discrepancy must be resolved before pre-registration.
- **Pre-registration date:** 2026-09-01 (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** PAM-13 change from baseline to 6 months
- **Secondary outcomes:** MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L, hospitalisation rate
- **Analysis plan:** ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity, Bonferroni correction for secondary endpoints

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
### Formula
n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²

### Parameters
- **Z_α/2:** 1.96 (α = 0.05, two-sided)
- **Z_β:** 0.84 (power = 0.80)
- **σ (pooled SD):** TBD (placeholder; pending confirmation from pilot data or literature; currently assumed 10 based on Hibbard 2005, but this requires verification)
- **δ (MCID):** 5.4 points (PAM-13)

### Computation
n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · 2 · σ² / (5.4)²

### Result
- **Per group:** N ≥ 55 (assuming σ = 10)
- **Total:** N ≥ 110
- **Sensitivity analysis:** TBD (placeholder; to be completed once σ is confirmed)

### Note
This calculation is a placeholder pending finalisation of σ. The value σ = 10 is used provisionally but requires confirmation from a systematic review or pilot data.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Low recruitment rate | Medium | High | Extend recruitment period; add recruitment sites |
| 2 | High dropout rate (>20%) | Medium | High | Implement retention strategies; per-protocol analysis |
| 3 | PAM-13 measurement error | Low | Medium | Use validated instrument; train assessors |
| 4 | Contamination between arms | Low | High | Cluster randomisation by site; monitor fidelity |
| 5 | Adverse events >5% | Low | High | DSMB monitoring; stopping rule in place |
| 6 | Data quality issues | Medium | Medium | Double data entry; automated validation checks |
| 7 | Software malfunction | Low | Medium | Backup systems; rollback plan |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Sample size:** The calculation relies on a placeholder σ (TBD), which may affect power if the true SD differs.
- **Generalisability:** Single-country study; results may not apply to other healthcare systems.
- **Blinding:** Open-label design; potential for performance bias.
- **Measurement:** PAM-13 is self-reported; subject to recall bias.
- **Duration:** 6-month follow-up may be insufficient to assess long-term outcomes.
- **Secondary outcomes:** Multiple comparisons without full correction may inflate Type I error.
- **Technology dependence:** Results may not generalise to other AI platforms or versions.
